title: Crispy Citrus Slovakian Stir-fry
description: This citrusy, crispy dish features truffle oil, deglazing with balsamic vinegar, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors.

ingredients.name: Cherry tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Use cherry tomatoes for added pop of color

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Extra virgin olive oil for added flavor and health benefits

ingredients.name: White wine vinegar
ingredients.notes: To deglaze the pan, making a quick reduction for extra depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Balsamic vinegar
ingredients.notes: For added tang and to deglaze the pan

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: To finish, adding a touch more for that golden-brown color and flavor

ingredients.name: Truffle oil
ingredients.notes: To elevate the dish with a subtle, earthy flavor

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Enhances the flavors of both tomatoes and truffle oil

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: To add color and slightly spicy notes

instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add cherry tomatoes and balsamic vinegar to deglaze the pan and reduce by half. Simmer for another minute or two till slightly thickened.
Stir in truffle oil just before adding the reduced tomato sauce back into the skillet. Toss well to combine.
Add Romaine leaves, tossing with the cherry tomato reduction until they wilt slightly but retain their crunch and color.
Top the wilting Romaine with finely grated Pecorino Romano cheese. Let it melt gently in the heat of the skillet.
Sprinkle fresh dill on top for garnish and a pop of freshness. Finish with a pinch of paprika to enhance its colors and subtle spiciness.
Serve hot, alongside crusty bread or as a side dish for your dinner party to impress everyone.
